items.briefing:
A critical flaw in Check Point VPN software is being actively exploited by the Qilin ransomware gang, with attacks dating back to early May. If your organisation uses Check Point VPN, ensure it has been patched immediately. Separately, Microsoft's systems were compromised to deliver malware targeting users of AI tools Claude and Gemini — if staff use these AI assistants, be cautious about any unexpected prompts or file downloads until further updates are available.

Iranian-linked hackers continue active operations despite a recent ceasefire agreement, and a US extortion campaign targeting law firms serves as a reminder that professional services organisations remain high-value targets globally, including in Australia.

**Practical tip:** Check with your IT team today whether your VPN software is up to date — unpatched VPNs are currently one of the most common ways attackers break into organisations.

featured.title: Microsoft Hacked to Deliver Malware to Claude and Gemini Users
featured.quote: Major cloud platform compromise threatens millions of AI users globally, including Australians.
